summary: Overnight-spot discovery PWA for campervans & motorcycles. Vanilla HTML/JS + Supabase + Vercel.
workflow_rules: No code changes without explicit user approval ('tamam'/'yap'/'yapalim'). Present diagnosis + plan first, then wait.
Repo is private: Claude cannot fetch source. User uploads files to /mnt/user-data/uploads; outputs to /mnt/user-data/outputs.
Use web_search + web_fetch for external libs/APIs/version-specific behavior; do not rely on model memory. Context7 connector declined for security.
Bump sw.js CACHE version (+1) whenever index.html changes. admin.html changes need no bump.
Env var changes require a Vercel redeploy.
Edit pattern: copy to /home/claude, Python with assert count==1, node --check largest inline script, copy to outputs, present_files.

stack.frontend: vanilla HTML/JS, Leaflet.js, single inline <script> in index.html (~340KB)

map.tile_resolution: per-style retina flag in MAP_STYLES; tileTemplate() adds @2x only when retina:true. street/outdoor=@2x (light PNG, crisp labels), satellite/hybrid=NO @2x (heavy JPEG -> 512 tile = ~4x less data, faster zoom). 512 vs @2x = same content scale (MapTiler), only DPI sharpness differs. sw TILES v1->v2 purges old 1024px satellite tiles on activate.

storage_buckets.name: place-photos
storage_buckets.reason: OpenAI must fetch image URL for moderation

supabase.place_enrichment_facilities: jsonb column added v41: {water,elec,wc,shower,wifi,dump,trash,shop,pool,sauna,jacuzzi,restaurant,bar,playground,laundry,naturist,beach,waterfront,waterfront_type,tents,caravans,pets,openfire,reservation,capacity,stars,motorcycle}. SQL: alter table place_enrichment add column if not exists facilities jsonb;

triggers.name: trg_enforce_photo_status
triggers.role: forces non-admin inserts to pending (security base; do not break)

triggers.name: trg_photo_report_hide
triggers.role: 3 distinct-user reports -> place_photos pending + admin_seen=false + mod_debug.community_hidden

api_endpoints./api/moderate: TEXT moderation: multilingual profanity blocklist (stem-based, TR agglutinative) + OpenAI omni. Returns {flagged, reason}
api_endpoints./api/moderate-photo: IMAGE moderation, server-authoritative. Triggered by moderate_photo webhook. Re-fetches row via service role, OpenAI omni image, score threshold, writes mod_debug. Clean->approved(auto_approved=true,admin_seen=false), else pending (fail-closed).
api_endpoints./api/enrich: place enrichment: reads the place OWN official website (Claude haiku-4-5 + web_search, never aggregators) for services(8)+price+summary+host+tariff_url+phone+email+images(re-hosted to Supabase) AND rich facilities(pool/sauna/jacuzzi/restaurant/bar/playground/laundry/naturist/beach/waterfront+type/tents/caravans/pets/openfire/reservation/capacity/stars/motorcycle) -> facilities jsonb. Yearly cache in place_enrichment. Caches ONLY on success; no_api_key/quota/ai_error/no_data -> no row. Env ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. {debug:true} POST returns env booleans. CURRENT BUILD find-site-2026-06-29d (adds photoOnly + refreshPhoto modes + server-side Mapillary fallback photo cache + writePhoto; see other_subsystems.photo_fallback_cache). Flow: direct multi-page site read PRIMARY (homepage+top-4 priority sub-pages via subpageScore where services+price/tarif=score0; metaText adds title/meta/og text), web_search FALLBACK; if no OSM website tag, search finds official host then reads THAT site directly for the tariff table. Price = lowest OVERNIGHT pitch/forfait, lowest season; excludes day-pass/membership/deposit/extra-person/electricity. temperature:0 on extraction = deterministic. Handler writeCaches ALL text info BEFORE photos, then re-hosts photos within a time budget (DEADLINE start+8500ms, Promise.race) so price/info never lost to a ~10s timeout. Cache row now stores name.

vercel_env.MODERATE_IMG_THRESHOLD: 0.2 (image risky-category score threshold; tune via mod_debug)

vercel_config.functions_note: moderate.js & moderate-photo.js need no maxDuration config (Pro/fluid gives high limit; log shows moderate-photo max 5m)

headers.purpose: prevent stale sw.js/HTML in browser HTTP cache so version bumps propagate automatically; sw version bump still required to clear the cache contents

moderation_pipeline.text: client submit -> moderateContent(text) -> /api/moderate -> clean=approved, flagged/error=pending (admin Yorumlar tab reviews)
moderation_pipeline.photo: upload -> place_photos pending (trigger) -> webhook moderate_photo -> /api/moderate-photo -> omni image + score>=threshold? pending : approved(auto). mod_debug records scores.
moderation_pipeline.community: 🚩 report button per user photo -> photo_reports insert (unique per user) -> 3 distinct users -> trigger sets photo pending + community_hidden. Admin Foto>Bekleyen shows red badge + reason breakdown.
moderation_pipeline.known_limitation: omni image scores are low for weapons/blood (~0.08-0.12); threshold catches violence/sexual but NOT relevance/quality -> community reports are the safety net.

other_subsystems.satimg_fallback_fix: SUPERSEDES satellite_v2_detail_fallback. satellite-v2 map id was INVALID -> request failed -> blank/placeholder P under Aerial-view badge. Fixed to match the working live tiles: satImg returns maps/satellite/static/{lng},{lat},16/600x400@2x.jpg (map id satellite, jpg, @2x, zoom 16). Verified live (loads 1200x800). index.html -> sw v63..v66.
other_subsystems.photo_fallback_cache: enrich.js BUILD 29c/d + index.html (sw v67): photoless places get a STABLE PERMANENT server-cached fallback photo instead of a different client Mapillary frame each reopen. NEW place_enrichment cols: photo(url), photo_src(mapillary/website/wikimedia), photo_exp(bigint unix sec from signed-URL oe= param), photo_ref(Mapillary image id). SQL: alter table place_enrichment add column if not exists photo text / photo_src text / photo_exp bigint / photo_ref text. STABLE KEY: skOf() for flagless places 's'+s.id (unstable every session -> cache never hit) -> 'g'+lat.toFixed(5)+'_'+lng.toFixed(5); osm/user/community keys unchanged. FLOW: applyCachedPhoto reads place_enrichment.photo on open; valid+unexpired -> use & skip client photo work; expired Mapillary -> POST /api/enrich {refreshPhoto:true} -> server refetches fresh URL for the SAME photo_ref id (Mapillary thumb URLs signed/TTL'd, must refetch by id); no photo -> POST /api/enrich {photoOnly:true} -> server resolves Mapillary deterministically (mapillaryByBbox nearest + tie-break smallest id) + writePhoto(). SECURITY: place_enrichment has ONLY a SELECT RLS policy (anon/authenticated), no INSERT/UPDATE -> clients can never write the shared cache; all writes service-role -> no cache-poisoning. writePhoto() upserts ONLY photo cols (merge-duplicates) so text row not clobbered. mapillaryNear() now dead. MAPILLARY_TOKEN added to enrich.js env.
other_subsystems.van_filter_removed_caravan_pin: v74: removed filt.vanOnly + the quickbar Van toggle entirely (the filter hid almost all parks in most areas due to sparse OSM motorhome/caravan/overnight tags -> looked broken/misleading). The modal van option was already dead code (const defined, never inserted into innerHTML); that const + .vopt handler were also cleaned up. vanOk/vanFromTags KEPT. Van-friendly parks are no longer hidden; instead they render a CARAVAN icon on the map pin: new ICONS.caravan (a large box-filling caravan: rounded body + roof vent + window + 2 wheels), catIcon uses svgIc(caravan) for van-friendly parks instead of svgIc(van). Pin color still encodes price status. (Shop pins still use the old van icon.)
other_subsystems.journals_ui_phase_j1: v87: active journal + name on top + switching. _journals/_activeJournalId globals. loadJournals() (added to init Promise.all) fetches the journals table, active = is_default (else first). loadVisits now tags s.journalId=v.journal_id (select * includes the column). visitedSpots() is now scoped to the active journal: s.visited && (journalId null || ===_activeJournalId) - null (session visit not yet tagged) counts as active so the mark-visited-shows-in-journal flow is not broken before J3. renderJournal header now shows the active journal name + a caret; tapping opens openJournalPicker() (journal list, checkmark = active). switchJournal(id) changes active + re-renders journal/markers/trip. This phase: read/show/switch (Add/rename/delete = J2; mark-visited writing into the active journal = J3). DB unchanged. index.html only -> sw v87.
other_subsystems.ui_attribution_position: v95: moved the Leaflet/MapTiler attribution (Leaflet | (c) MapTiler (c) OpenStreetMap contributors) to sit just above the bottom tab bar. It was at .leaflet-bottom.leaflet-right{bottom:140px} (raised to clear the old bottom-right fabs), leaving it floating mid-map. other_subsystems.hero_src_unified_note: v114: closed the remaining display paths that still showed s.heroPhoto (OSM/web/wiki-fed): renderList thumb, pvCardHtml map-popup hero, renderComparePanel + renderCompareTable thumbs. New helper heroSrc(s) (right after detailImages) applies the SAME rule everywhere: visitor photo (userPhotos/pendingPhotos) else satellite-v2 z18 for camp/park else placeholder. heroPhoto assignments remain (enrichment writes them) but NO UI reads them anymore. Also: local-only add-place session preview now goes to s.userPhotos=[objectURL] instead of heroPhoto so the user still sees their own photo. This was why v113 still showed the village shot - the big image on the card was pvCardHtml heroPhoto, not the carousel. index.html -> sw v114.
other_subsystems.visitor_photos_only_note: v113: detailImages() real-photo definition NARROWED to visitor photos only (userPhotos + pendingPhotos). OSM image= (osmPhoto) and camp-website images (webImages) no longer displayed anywhere in the carousel - third-party copyright risk + frequently irrelevant town/landscape shots (e.g. Jugendzeltplatz Mittermarchenbach showed a village vista). Display rule is now strictly: visitor photos if any, else satellite (satellite-v2 z18) for camp/park, else placeholder. Enrichment still stores osmPhoto/webImages fields (harmless, UI ignores). Legal note: satellite licensed via MapTiler subscription; visitor photos licensed via Terms; dropped sources were the only grey area. index.html -> sw v113.
other_subsystems.satellite_hero_note: v112: place-detail imagery simplified to REAL PHOTOS or SATELLITE, nothing else. satImg() upgraded: style satellite -> satellite-v2 (current Maxar/aerial, much sharper in Europe) and zoom 16 -> 18 (site actually fills the 600x400@2x frame). detailImages() fallback chain: Mapillary (mapPhoto) + Wikipedia (wikiPhoto) REMOVED from display (they often showed irrelevant street/town shots); enrichment may still store those fields but UI ignores them. New logic: real photos (visitor uploads + OSM image= + camp own-website webImages) win; while enriching -> placeholder; settled & photoless -> satellite for cat camp OR park (was camp-only), others placeholder. Aerial badge unchanged. Quota: 1 static request per photoless camp/park detail open - fine at current traffic, watch at scale. index.html -> sw v112.
other_subsystems.pro_modal_full_features_note: v111: renderUpgrade Pro modal feature list expanded from 3 route-only bullets to 6 covering ALL Pro-gated features. The app gates 8 things on isPro (route planning/enterRoute, optimizeRoute, exportGpx, openNavChooser, openVehicle vehicle-profile, openCompare, openNearby, findRoadsideHelp, saveAreaOffline); the modal previously listed only route planning/optimization/navigation. New 6 bullets (helpers merged): (1) Vehicle-aware route planning (multi-stop sized to vehicle + road-distance optimization + total time), (2) One-tap navigation (Google Maps/Waze/GPX), (3) Compare campsites, (4) Nearby travellers, (5) Roadside help, (6) Save areas offline. Ordered to lead with the vehicle-fit value spine. NOTE: inline translation is NOT Pro (free for all) so not listed. index.html -> sw v111.
other_subsystems.pricing_parametric_monthly_note: v110: Pro pricing made parametric + monthly plan added to the purchase UI. New consts (near PRO_CHECKOUT_URL): PRICE_YEAR='€24.99', PRICE_MONTH='€4.99'; single PRO_CHECKOUT_URL split into PRO_CHECKOUT_URL_YEAR + PRO_CHECKOUT_URL_MONTH (both placeholders until LS products exist). checkoutUrl(plan) picks the URL by plan ('month' vs default year) and still appends checkout[email]+custom[user_id]. renderUpgrade now shows TWO options: primary annual button (€24.99/year) with a 'Best value · save ~58% vs monthly' sub-label, and a secondary ghost 'or €4.99/month' button; proHero subtitle parametric. Account upgrade link -> 'Upgrade to Pro — from €4.99/month'. Onboarding wizard slide (index.html ~978 + its PREWARM_SRC translation mirror) had ' Just €20/year.' REMOVED (price lives only at the purchase point; keeps a single price source + avoids translation-cache drift). All €20 literals gone. STILL TODO (payment step): real LS checkout URLs, EU 14-day withdrawal-consent checkbox before redirect, legal-page links in the modal. index.html -> sw v110.
other_subsystems.nearby_reply_hidden_own_note: v109: in Nearby travelers the ↩ Reply button on a POST is now hidden on the user's OWN post (mine) - replying to yourself is pointless. buildNearbyRows post action row: the Reply button wrapped in ${mine?'':`...`} so it shows only on others' posts (alongside Block); own posts show just Edit/Delete. Reply count badge goes with it (replies still render below). Own replies keep their own Edit/Delete. index.html -> sw v109.
other_subsystems.nearby_owner_edit_note: v108: Nearby travelers users can now edit & delete their OWN posts and replies. buildNearbyRows renders Edit/Delete buttons where mine/rmine is true (Block shows only for others). New: nbEditOpen state + nbEditBox(id,body,fs) inline textarea (Cancel/Save); editNearby/cancelEditNearby/saveEditNearby (optimistic body update then supa.from('nearby_posts').update({body}).eq('id',id).eq('user_id',authUser.id), rollback+toast on error); deleteNearby (openConfirm danger -> optimistic splice of the post + its replies -> .delete().eq('id',id).eq('user_id',authUser.id), rollback on error). REQUIRES RLS: nearby_owner_edit_delete.sql adds owner UPDATE + DELETE policies (auth.uid()=user_id) on nearby_posts - without them update/delete silently affect 0 rows. index.html -> sw v108; SQL run separately in Supabase.
other_subsystems.nav_custom_start_note: v107: clarified navigation behaviour for the v106 custom route start. Verified via web search of the Waze deep-link API + Google's Navigation Connect docs: Waze and Sygic deep links accept only a DESTINATION (ll/q / coordinate) - no origin param - so they always navigate from the driver's current GPS location; you cannot force a custom start. Google Maps dir URL (routeGoogleUrl: origin=routePoints()[0]=routeStartPt()) and GPX (buildGpx uses routePoints) both correctly honour the picked start. No code change could make Waze/Sygic use a custom origin. Fix = UX: openNavChooser now shows an amber warning when routeStart is a custom map point - Google Maps & GPX use it; Waze & Sygic navigate from current location. index.html only -> sw v107.
other_subsystems.route_start_pickable: v106: route-plan start is no longer hard-locked to My location - it DEFAULTS to My location but can be picked from the map. New session state: routeStart (null = My location default; {lat,lng,name} = map-picked) + startPicking flag. New helper routeStartPt() returns routeStart||userLoc; routePoints() and the optimize/route API startPt now use it (were hard-coded to userLoc). renderRoutePanel start row rebuilt: shows 'Start: My location' or 'Start: Map point' with a '🗺 Pick on map' button (pickRouteStart -> startPicking + crosshair + live hint) and, when custom, a '↺ My location' reset (resetRouteStart). Map click handler gains a startPicking branch that sets routeStart from the tapped latlng, clears routeMeta, redraws. drawPlan already uses routePoints so the line follows the new start. clearRoute + exitRoutePick reset routeStart/startPicking. Scope: session-only (reload -> back to My location; stops still persist). pickRouteStart is Pro-gated (route planning already is). CSS: .rp-start flex justify-between + .rp-startbtn/.rp-startacts. index.html only -> sw v106.
other_subsystems.tab_icons_round_plus_zoom: v105: two tweaks. (1) Bottom tab icons restyled to match the top .rfab buttons: .tab svg now sits in a white circle (background var(--card), border-radius:50%, box-shadow 0 3px 10px, padding:11px around a 24px icon ~= 46px circle, color #374151), replacing the v104 white drop-shadow halo; .tab.on svg -> pine. Labels kept below (still floating with text-shadow). PRO badge (.tab-pro) moved to the circle's top-right (top:6px,left:calc(50% + 11px)+shadow). (2) Bottom-left zoom control fix: map is created with zoomControl:false but a zoom control is manually added via L.control.zoom({position:'bottomleft'}) (line ~1142) - it was sitting under the bottom-left 'Near me' tab icon. Raised it above the floating tabs with .leaflet-bottom.leaflet-left{bottom:calc(96px + safe-area);z-index:1040}. index.html only -> sw v105.
other_subsystems.tabbar_transparent: v104: bottom tab bar made transparent - the white background, shadow and rounded top corners removed so the 5 tab icons (Near me/Search/Add/Compare/Menu) float directly over the map. .tabbar: background:var(--card)->transparent, box-shadow:none, dropped border-top radii, + pointer-events:none so the empty gaps between icons pass touches through to the map (pan). .tab: pointer-events:auto (stay tappable) + color #5b6b7a->#37474f, weight 600->700, and a white text-shadow halo for legibility over any map. .tab svg: white drop-shadow halo. Icons kept; only the bar chrome removed (per user). index.html only -> sw v104.
other_subsystems.default_cats_stay_only: v103: default visible categories reduced to the 4 'park-able/stay' ones so the initial view + area search isn't cluttered with every POI. New const STAY_CATS=['park','camp','wild','picnic']. Changes: (1) initial activeCats = new Set(STAY_CATS) (was new Set(Object.keys(CATS)) = all 7); (2) searchArea auto-reveal only adds a result category if STAY_CATS.includes(k) — so shop/service/repair no longer auto-appear from an area search; (3) cache-restore add() likewise gated by STAY_CATS; (4) Filters Reset restores STAY_CATS (was all 7). shop/service/repair are now opt-in via Filters -> Categories (all 7 still listed there). KEPT (intentional exceptions): user's own added/favorite/journal/user spots still activeCats.add(their cat) so saved items stay visible; findRoadsideHelp still activeCats.add('repair') when the user requests roadside help; a name-search hit still adds its cat. Speeds up first selection (fewer pins, stay spots foregrounded). index.html only -> sw v103.
other_subsystems.moto_filter_shops: v102: when the motorcycle-friendly filter is on, SHOPS are now restricted to motorcycle shops only. spotPassesFilters moto block extended: in addition to camps requiring s.moto, `if(s.cat==='shop' && s.shopType!=='moto') return false;` so caravan/camping shops are hidden and only shopType==='moto' shops show. Other categories (park/picnic/service/etc.) still pass through unaffected. index.html only -> sw v102.
other_subsystems.compare_camp_only: v101: 'Add to compare' is now restricted to CAMPSITES only (was camp/service/park). Only campsites can be compared with each other. Three edits: (1) detail-card button gate cmpEligible = ['camp','service','park'].includes(s.cat) -> s.cat==='camp'; (2) pin-flow predicate cmpStay = s.cat==='camp' (drives promptAddToCompare popup + addToCompare guard), so tapping a non-camp pin in compare-pick mode shows a disabled button; (3) that disabled label changed 'Not a place to stay' -> 'Only campsites'. NOTE: two other ['camp','service','park'] checks (lines ~1854, ~2026) are enrichment/'stay' loading-state gates, unrelated to compare, left untouched. index.html only -> sw v101.
other_subsystems.moto_filter_camp_only: v100: the motorcycle-friendly filter now only constrains CAMPSITES, mirroring how fitOnly only checks parking spots. spotPassesFilters: `if(filt.motoOnly && !s.moto)` -> `if(filt.motoOnly && s.cat==='camp' && !s.moto) return false;`. So with the moto filter on, non-camp categories (park/picnic/etc.) pass through unaffected; only camps are restricted to moto-friendly ones. index.html only -> sw v100.
other_subsystems.vehicle_type_moto_filter: v99: vehicle profile gains a TYPE (Campervan vs Motorcycle) + a motorcycle-friendly filter. vehicle object: added type:'van' (default) to the declaration and to loadVehicleRemote's Object.assign default; stored in the existing profiles.vehicle JSON blob (NO schema change). UI (both editors - Account #ac-* and the Pro Vehicle-profile modal #v-*): a .veh-type selector [Campervan|Motorcycle] whose buttons toggle .on; the height/length/weight fields + vehicle-aware-routing toggle are wrapped in #ac-vandims / #v-vandims and hidden (display:none) when Motorcycle is chosen (dimensions are irrelevant for a bike). Save handlers read the selected type from .vt-opt.on. Filter: new motoFromTags(t) sets a per-spot boolean s.moto from OSM tags (motorcycle:theme / motorcycle_friendly / motorcycle = yes) at newSpot creation, and enrichment also sets s.moto=true when web facilities JSON has .motorcycle. filt gained motoOnly (default+reset); filtersActive/filterCount include it; spotPassesFilters adds `if(filt.motoOnly && !s.moto) return false;`. In Filters -> Quick, the chip is conditional: when vehicle.type==='moto' show a '🏍️ Motorcycle friendly' chip (toggles filt.motoOnly), otherwise the existing 'Fits my vehicle' chip. Added .veh-type/.vt-opt CSS. NOTE: moto-friendly data is sparse (depends on OSM/web tags) so few results is expected. NOTE(process): a failed open('w') with surrogate-pair emoji escapes truncated index.html to 0 bytes mid-session; restored from outputs (v98) and switched the edit script to atomic write (tmp -> os.replace) + single-codepoint \U escapes. index.html only -> sw v99.
other_subsystems.ui_logo_order_fix: v98: mobile bug - logo appeared on the RIGHT of the button cluster instead of the left. Root cause: a leftover mobile media query from the old topbar sets .brand{order:1} (line ~114); since the logo element still carries class 'brand' and is now a flex child of .fab-corner, order:1 pushed it to the END on mobile widths (desktop had no such rule, so it looked fine there). other_subsystems.ui_single_corner_cluster: v97: per feedback, all four corner buttons now live in ONE cluster beside the logo (was 2-left/2-right in v96). Merged by deleting the tl-close + tr-open between listFab and styleFab, so .fab-corner.tl = [logo][Favorites][List][Map style][Filters] in a single top-left flex row; the .fab-corner.tr element is gone (its CSS rule is now dead but harmless). .fab-corner gap 10->8px for a tighter single-group look. The <=380px media (hide subtitle, wordmark 14.5, .rfab 44px) still guards narrow-screen fit. index.html only -> sw v97.
other_subsystems.ui_logo_inline_row: v96: per feedback, the top-left icons now sit on the SAME row as the logo (were below it in v94). other_subsystems.enrich_vision_photo_filter: enrich.js BUILD=vision-photos-2026-06-28: replaced fragile filename/dimension photo heuristics with VISION-based selection. New pipeline rehostImagesSmart: fetchImageItem (parallel fetch up to 6 candidates, dimension+size guard via imgDims) -> aiClassifyPhotos (sends each image to claude-haiku-4-5 as a vision input, model returns JSON bool[] keeping only genuine place photographs, dropping logos/icons/maps/illustrations/banners; null on failure keeps dimension-passed set) -> uploadBuffers (uploads kept buffers to place-photos/enrich/<key>/N). Handler now calls rehostImagesSmart(images,key,apiKey). Every response (debug/cached/success) includes build:BUILD so deploy is verifiable. Old rehostImages kept but unused. Note: adds up to 6 image fetches + 1 vision call per enrichment (cached yearly); watch Vercel timeout. Candidate cap reduced 6->3 (BUILD vision-photos-2026-06-28b) to roughly halve vision token cost; ~1 vision call/place/year, well under $0.01 each. CACHE-BUST FIX (BUILD 28c): rehosted URLs now get ?v=<timestamp> (uploadBuffers) - same filenames (0.png..) had cache-control max-age=1yr, so overwriting content did NOT refresh in browser/CDN (root cause of repeatedly seeing the same logo even after re-enrich). Unique ?v per run defeats stale cache.
other_subsystems.enrich_image_quality_filter: enrich.js: rehostImages now reads real pixel dimensions from image header bytes (imgDims, no deps; PNG/JPEG/WebP/GIF) and drops logos/icons/illustrations/banners by size (min side <400px or aspect >3) instead of only byte size; unknown-dims fall back to 20KB byte floor. junk URL regex extended (kalender/calendar/reservier/reservation/buchung/booking/illustration/platzhalter/vorschau + flaticon/freepik/iconfinder/pngtree CDNs). Fixes campsite logo/calendar/placeholder appearing as photos. enrich.js change only — no sw bump needed.
other_subsystems.find_site_for_website_less_camps: enrich.js BUILD find-site-2026-06-29b: a camp with NO OSM website tag (e.g. oway155180328, WEBSITE=undefined) returned price NULL because enrichOnePlace skipped the reliable direct multi-page read (needs a url) and fell back to aiWithSearch, whose web-search snippets miss the tariffs table. Fix: after aiWithSearch discovers the official site (sets result.host) and no direct bundle was read, enrichOnePlace now reads that official site DIRECTLY (fetchSiteBundle on https://<host> + aiFromText) to get the reliable tariffs/facilities, then backfills any empty field from the search result. So website-less camps whose official site is findable now get the tariff-table price. If search cannot find an official site (host stays null) the price is legitimately null. Note: this path runs two AI calls (search + direct read) so it is slower on first enrich, but the text-first cache (29a) writes the price before photos, so nothing is lost and later opens are instant. enrich.js only.
other_subsystems.text_first_before_photos: enrich.js BUILD text-first-2026-06-29a: app showed price unknown after the stale 15.90 row was deleted, while the live console test (skipPhotos:true) returned the correct 21.70. Root cause: the app sends skipPhotos:!!s.osmPhoto, so on a camp with no OSM photo it runs the FULL photo pipeline (fetch+vision+rehost) which can exceed the Vercel ~10s limit -> the whole function times out -> ALL info (price/services/facilities) is lost and nothing is cached. Fix: handler now persists ALL textual info FIRST (writeCache with images emptied) the moment enrichOnePlace returns, BEFORE the photo pipeline; then re-hosts photos within a strict time budget (DEADLINE = start+8500ms, Promise.race) and upgrades the cached row only if photos finish in time. So price/info ALWAYS returns and is cached even when photos overrun. Also: result.name is now set from the request (was null) so the cache row is identifiable - this is why an earlier delete ... where name ilike %hesp% matched nothing. enrich.js only.
other_subsystems.price_deterministic: enrich.js BUILD price-deterministic-2026-06-28k: live test of Les Hesperides returned correct from 21.70 EUR/night but the in-app run returned from 15.90 EUR/night (a figure on NONE of the real overnight pages - both the tariffs table and the WooCommerce product page list Emplacement Camping Moyenne 21,70 / Haute 31,00). Root cause: NON-DETERMINISM - the model gave different answers per run (15.90 likely from a day-pass product or hallucinated). Fix: temperature:0 on extraction calls aiFromText and aiWithSearch -> deterministic, consistently rule-compliant (lowest OVERNIGHT pitch, day-pass/membership excluded). Pairs with 28j price rules + price-page priority. enrich.js only. Verify via live console test (should stably show 21.70), then delete stale cache by name/place_key NOT host (old failed rows can have host=null and escape a host filter).
other_subsystems.price_extraction_rules: enrich.js BUILD price-rules-2026-06-28j: fixed overnight from-price coming back null on camps whose tariff page lists multiple price types (e.g. Les Hesperides: day-visitor 16,70€ + FFN/FFCC membership 51€ + nightly 2-person forfait by season). (A) SCHEMA price rule rewritten: take the LOWEST nightly OVERNIGHT price (tent/pitch emplacement or cheapest rental); use the package/forfait figure; if seasons shown (Moyenne/Haute, Low/High) use the LOWEST season; explicitly IGNORE day-visitor/day-pass/entry fees, club membership/licence/federation fees (FFN, FFCC, annual dues, timbre), deposits, extra-person supplements, electricity/option add-ons. So Les Hesperides -> from 21.70€/night (Emplacement Camping, Moyenne) not 16,70€ day-pass or 51€ membership. (B) subpageScore: price/tarif pages promoted to score 0 (same top priority as services), so the tariffs page is never dropped from the 4-subpage crawl on amenity-heavy sites (verified Reservations & Tarifs ranks first for Les Hesperides). enrich.js only, no sw/index bump. other_subsystems.mapillary_wiki_fallback_only: v57: detailImages reworked so Mapillary (street-level) and Wikipedia (often a town/region shot) are FALLBACK-ONLY.
